name: ARGB LED Matrix
fullName: WS2812B Addressable RGB LED Matrix Panel
tagline: Individually addressable full-color RGB LED matrix — each LED is independently programmable
description: An ARGB (Addressable RGB) LED matrix is a grid of WS2812B LEDs where each LED contains its own driver chip and can be set to any color independently using a single data wire. Common sizes are 8×8 (64 LEDs) and 16×16 (256 LEDs). They're used for scrolling text displays, pixel art animations, game visualizations, and ambient lighting. The FastLED and Adafruit NeoPixel libraries make control straightforward.

specs.currentPerLED: ~60mA per LED (full white = R+G+B all at 255)
specs.ledType: WS2812B (integrated IC + RGB LED in single package)
specs.colorDepth: 24-bit color (8 bits per channel, 16.7 million colors)

pinout.name: Power
pinout.desc: 5V DC — use an external 5V power supply with sufficient amperage (not Arduino 5V pin)

pinout.name: Ground
pinout.desc: Shared ground with Arduino and power supply

pinout.name: Data In
pinout.desc: Single data wire — connect to an Arduino digital pin (e.g., D6) via a 330–500Ω resistor

wiringNote: ALWAYS use an external 5V power supply — never power more than a few LEDs from the Arduino's 5V pin. Add a 1000µF capacitor across the 5V and GND lines to prevent power surge on startup. Use a 330Ω–500Ω resistor in series on the DATA line to protect WS2812B from ringing.
libraryInstall: Arduino IDE → Library Manager → Search 'FastLED' by Daniel Garcia → Install. OR search 'Adafruit NeoPixel' → Install.
commonMistakes: Powering from Arduino 5V pin → Arduino resets or burns when too many LEDs are on
Not adding a 330Ω resistor on DATA line → signal integrity issues, random color flickering
Setting brightness to 255 with all LEDs white → draws massive current, causes power supply brownout
Not sharing GND between external supply and Arduino → data signal doesn't reference correctly
Forgetting 1000µF capacitor → voltage spike on power-on can destroy the first LED in the chain
